introduction: A common presentation in ENT emergency practice; commoner in children than adults. Children may inadvertently insert various objects (beads, plastic toys, seeds etc) into their ears while playing. Live insects may also crawl into the ear in adults/children.

features: Mild pain; sensation of blockage in older children
Object usually seen with good light in the ear canal

goals: Remove object expeditiously without damage to ear structures or causing undue pain to patient
non_drug: Removal with appropriate hook, or alligator forceps
Examination and removal under anaesthesia if difficult in the clinic

complications: Perforation of tympanic membrane from attempts at removal
